skill1.kitLine: Activates when performing a Full Charge attack. Affects the enemy unit nearest to the crosshair. Deals 68.46% of final ATK as sustained damage every 1 sec, stacks up to 10 times and lasts for 5 sec.
skill1.where: skill1[0] (trigger shotFired → target enemy → dot atkPct 68.46, intervalSec 1, durationSec 5, flavor sustained)
skill1.note: shotFired is the full-charge proxy (every RL pull in-engine is a full-charge shot). The 10-stack cap is NOT encodable on a dot effect but is documented as NON-BINDING at datamined cadence (~2.8 concurrent 5s instances, peak 3.7 < 10). Cadence/stacking is an unmeasured ⚑ — see confounds.

skill1.kitLine: Activates when entering Full Burst. Affects self. ATK ▲ 47.52% of the skill user's ATK for 10 sec.
skill1.where: skill1[1] (trigger fullBurstEnter → target self → buff casterAtkPct 47.52, durationSec 10)
skill1.note: Literal 'when entering Full Burst' → fullBurstEnter; 'of the skill user's ATK' → casterAtkPct. Faithful.

skill2.kitLine: Activates at the start of battle. Affects self. Vital Attack: Damage to Parts ▲ 21.12% for 5 sec.
skill2.where: skill2[0] (trigger passive → target self → buff partsDamagePct 21.12, durationSec 5)
skill2.note: Battle-start trigger encoded as `passive` (slight approximation of literal 'start of battle'). INERT vs the partless scope-lock boss but kept per hard-rule-3 (never delete a stat buff a future parts consumer could use).

skill2.kitLine: Activates when entering Full Burst. Affects self. Vital Attack: Damage to Parts ▲ 21.12% for 5 sec.
skill2.where: skill2[1] (trigger fullBurstEnter → target self → buff partsDamagePct 21.12, durationSec 5)
skill2.note: INERT vs partless boss; kept for the same future-consumer reason.

skill2.kitLine: Activates when an ally or self destroys an enemy's part. Affects self if self is not in A.N. Mode status.
skill2.note: Part-destroy trigger can never fire on the partless scope-lock boss (genuinely-skippable class: parts); no part-destroy trigger exists in types.ts either.

skill2.kitLine: Single Point Attack: Sustained damage ▲ 47.32% for 15 sec.
skill2.note: Gated behind the part-destroy trigger above, so it never fires vs partless boss. Deliberately REPLACES a prior hand-authored always-on sustainedDamagePct 47.32 passive that assumed destructible parts (contradicted the scope-lock partless basis; was flagged UNCERTAIN/never measured).
Removal lives on the same never-firing part-destroy branch; no-op vs partless boss.

burst.kitLine: Affects all enemies (including parts). Deals 492.3% of final ATK as Burst Skill damage.
burst.where: burst[0] (trigger burstCast → target enemy → flatDamage atkPct 492.3)
burst.note: 'all enemies (including parts)' collapses to the one boss; auto FB-exempt (noFb never set on burst-cast). Faithful.

The A.N. Mode header carries no independent numeric effect; its only children are Effect 1 (removal, a no-op here) and Effect 2 (modeled separately as a stat buff). Mode granted in her OWN burst block → burstCast per note.

burst.kitLine: A.N. Mode Effect 1: Removes Single Point Attack.
burst.note: Single Point Attack never exists vs the partless boss, so the removal is a no-op; the old double-count concern with the prior 47.32 passive dissolves with it.

burst.kitLine: A.N. Mode Effect 2: Sustained damage ▲ 89.44% for 10 sec.
burst.where: burst[1] (trigger burstCast → target self → buff sustainedDamagePct 89.44, durationSec 10)
burst.note: Mode granted in her own burst block → burstCast (NOT fullBurstEnter) per note. Faithful encoding of the numeric effect.

documentedConfounds.text: PARSER BASELINE banner: entire override is a HYPOTHESIS, never measured/hand-tuned; tier MODEL_ONLY, 'Pure model — never fielded'. Remove banner only once measured.
documentedConfounds.why: No part of this kit has been validated against a real fight; any number could drift. Corroborated by empty board data (no ratio/N/MAD).

documentedConfounds.text: skill1: full-charge DoT cadence (60f charge + 22f bolt gap + 141f reload, autofire unverified) is an unmeasured ⚑ estimate.
documentedConfounds.why: The S1 DoT is her DOMINANT damage bucket (~1.9×ATK/s vs ~0.35×ATK/s weapon) and scales LINEARLY with shot rate, so a cadence miss moves total damage proportionally. Top-priority ⚑.

documentedConfounds.text: note ⚑(1): cadence tuple — charge 60f / bolt-gap 22f default ON (autofire NOT set; noBoltRecovery would be a ~15-20% shot swing) / reloadFrames 141 / rolling-reload unknown; estimate ~0.57 shots/s.
documentedConfounds.why: Each unverified component (autofire, bolt recovery, rolling reload) directly changes shots/s and thus DoT instances; the autofire/bolt assumption alone is a ~15-20% swing on the dominant bucket.

documentedConfounds.text: note ⚑(2): S1 DoT stacking vs cadence — confirm per-shot independent 5s instances and that the 10-cap never binds; expect ≈3 × 68.46% popups/s steady-state.
documentedConfounds.why: The faithful repeating-trigger encoding appends an independent instance per shot; if real stacking/cap behavior differs, the dominant bucket is miscounted.

documentedConfounds.text: note ⚑(3): DoT Full-Burst timing — default FB-by-timing ON (no noFb); verify +50% on in-FB ticks.
documentedConfounds.why: A ±50% modifier on the dominant DoT bucket during Full Burst windows is a large swing if the default timing assumption is wrong.

documentedConfounds.text: note ⚑(4): RL projectile split — hitsPerShot 1, single instance assumed; verify via popups.
documentedConfounds.why: If the RL shot splits into multiple instances, the shotFired DoT trigger count (dominant bucket) changes.

documentedConfounds.text: skill2: Single Point Attack (Sustained damage ▲ 47.32%) keys on destroying an enemy part — never fires vs the partless scope-lock boss; not modeled (previously approximated as an always-on passive).
documentedConfounds.why: Genuinely inert under the partless scope-lock basis, so excluding it is correct there; it would only matter on a destructible-parts boss, which is out of the modeled scope. Removing the old always-on passive is a deliberate correction, not a silent drop.

documentedConfounds.text: skill2: Vital Attack (Damage to Parts ▲ 21.12%) is modeled but inert in v1 (no parts on the boss).
documentedConfounds.why: partsDamagePct has no consumer on a partless boss, so the modeled buff contributes zero either way — kept only for future parts consumers.

authorIntentSummary: The author produced a PARSER-BASELINE HYPOTHESIS (explicitly not measured/hand-tuned; unit never fielded, no board data). Skill1 is modeled as a stacking sustained DoT (68.46%/s, 5s, one independent instance per full-charge shot via shotFired) — her dominant damage bucket — plus a Full-Burst-enter self ATK buff (47.52%, 10s). Skill2 keeps both Vital Attack partsDamagePct 21.12 buffs as inert-but-retained stat buffs, while the part-destroy branch (Single Point Attack sustained ▲47.32 + 'Removes Vital Attack') is deliberately UNMODELED because it can never fire on the partless scope-lock boss — a conscious replacement of a prior uncertain always-on passive. Burst is a 492.3% nuke plus self sustained ▲89.44% (10s) on burstCast, with the A.N. Mode header and Effect-1 removal unmodeled as no-ops. The known-not-faithful spot is the entire DoT cadence/stacking/FB-timing/projectile-split complex (⚑ list 1-4), flagged as top priority for hand-tuning since it scales the dominant bucket linearly.
